item.text: I’ll inspect the TLA+ module and existing lemmas, then iteratively patch the final proof and run TLAPS/checks.

item.aggregated_output:
------------ MODULE AtomicBakeryWithoutSMT_InitInv ----------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* The Atomic Bakery algorithm is a version of the bakery algorithm in     *)
(* which reading and writing of a process's number is an atomic operation. *)
(* The bakery algorithm originally appeared in:                            *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*   Leslie Lamport                                                        *)
(*   A New Solution of Dijkstra's Concurrent Programming Problem           *)
(*   Communications of the ACM 17, 8   (August 1974), 453-455              *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* This PlusCal version of the Atomic Bakery algorithm is one in which     *)
(* variables whose initial values are not used are initialized to          *)
(* arbitrary type-correct values.  It is good for proving correctness but  *)
(* not for model checking, because it produces an unnecessarily large      *)
(* number of reachable states.  If the variables were left uninitialized,  *)
(* the PlusCal translation would initialize them to a particular           *)
(* unspecified value.  This would complicate the proof because it would    *)
(* make the type-correctness invariant more complicated, but it would be   *)
(* efficient to model check.  We could write a version that is both easy   *)
(* to prove and efficient to model check by initializing the variables to  *)
(* particular type-correct values.                                         *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The proofs in this module were written before TLAPS's SMT backend was   *)
(* implemented, and constitute indeed one of the first proofs ever carried *)
(* out using TLAPS. Much shorter proofs can be obtained using that backend *)
(* -- see module AtomicBakery in the same directory.                      *)
(***************************************************************************)
EXTENDS Naturals, TLAPS

CONSTANT P
ASSUME PsubsetNat == P \subseteq Nat

(*********************************************************************
--algorithm AtomicBakery {
variable num = [i \in P |-> 0], flag = [i \in P |-> FALSE];

process (p \in P)
  variables unread \in SUBSET P, 
            max \in Nat, 
            nxt \in P
{
p1: while (TRUE) {
      unread := P \ {self} ;
      max := 0;
      flag[self] := TRUE;
p2:   while (unread # {}) {
        with (i \in unread) { unread := unread \ {i};
                              if (num[i] > max) { max := num[i]; }
         }
       };
p3:   num[self] := max + 1;
p4:   flag[self] := FALSE;
      unread := P \ {self} ;
p5:   while (unread # {}) {
        with (i \in unread) { nxt := i ; };
        await ~ flag[nxt];
p6:     await \/ num[nxt] = 0
              \/ IF self > nxt THEN num[nxt] > num[self]
                               ELSE num[nxt] \geq num[self];
        unread := unread \ {nxt};
        } ;
p7:   skip ; \* critical section;
p8:  num[self] := 0;
 }}
}
*********************************************************************)

\* BEGIN TRANSLATION
CONSTANT defaultInitValue
VARIABLES num, flag, pc, unread, max, nxt

vars == << num, flag, pc, unread, max, nxt >>

Init == (* Global variables *)
        /\ num = [i \in P |-> 0]
        /\ flag = [i \in P |-> FALSE]
        (* Process p *)
        /\ unread \in [P -> SUBSET P]
        /\ max \in [P -> Nat]
        /\ nxt \in [P -> P]
        /\ pc = [self \in P |-> "p1"]

p1(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p1"
            /\ unread' = [unread EXCEPT ![self] = P \ {self}]
            /\ max' = [max EXCEPT ![self] = 0]
            /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![self] = TRUE]
            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p2"]
            /\ UNCHANGED << num, nxt >>

p2(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p2"
            /\ IF unread[self] # {}
                  THEN /\ \E i \in unread[self]:
                            /\ unread' = [unread EXCEPT
                                            ![self] = unread[self] \ {i}]
                            /\ IF num[i] > max[self]
                                  THEN /\ max' = [max EXCEPT
                                                    ![self] = num[i]]
                                  ELSE /\ TRUE
                                       /\ UNCHANGED max
                       /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p2"]
                  ELSE /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p3"]
                       /\ UNCHANGED << unread, max >>
            /\ UNCHANGED << num, flag, nxt >>

p3(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p3"
            /\ num' = [num EXCEPT ![self] = max[self] + 1]
            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p4"]
            /\ UNCHANGED << flag, unread, max, nxt >>

p4(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p4"
            /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![self] = FALSE]
            /\ unread' = [unread EXCEPT ![self] = P \ {self}]
            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p5"]
            /\ UNCHANGED << num, max, nxt >>

p5(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p5"
            /\ IF unread[self] # {}
                  THEN /\ \E i \in unread[self]:
                            nxt' = [nxt EXCEPT
                                      ![self] = i]
                       /\ ~ flag[nxt'[self]]
                       /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p6"]
                  ELSE /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p7"]
                       /\ UNCHANGED nxt
            /\ UNCHANGED << num, flag, unread, max >>

p6(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p6"
            /\ \/ num[nxt[self]] = 0
               \/ IF self > nxt[self] THEN num[nxt[self]] > num[self]
                                      ELSE num[nxt[self]] >= num[self]
            /\ unread' = [unread EXCEPT ![self] = unread[self] \ {nxt[self]}]
            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p5"]
            /\ UNCHANGED << num, flag, max, nxt >>

p7(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p7"
            /\ TRUE
            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p8"]
            /\ UNCHANGED << num, flag, unread, max, nxt >>

p8(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p8"
            /\ num' = [num EXCEPT ![self] = 0]
            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p1"]
            /\ UNCHANGED << flag, unread, max, nxt >>

p(self) == p1(self) \/ p2(self) \/ p3(self) \/ p4(self) \/ p5(self)
              \/ p6(self) \/ p7(self) \/ p8(self)

Next == (\E self \in P: p(self))
           \/ (* Disjunct to prevent deadlock on termination *)
              ((\A self \in P: pc[self] = "Done") /\ UNCHANGED vars)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars

Termination == <>(\A self \in P: pc[self] = "Done")

\* END TRANSLATION

MutualExclusion == \A i,j \in P : (i # j) => ~ /\ pc[i] = "p7"
                                               /\ pc[j] = "p7"

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
TypeOK == /\ num  \in [P -> Nat]
          /\ flag \in [P -> BOOLEAN]
          /\ unread \in [P -> SUBSET P]
          /\ \A i \in P :
                pc[i] \in {"p2", "p5", "p6"} => i \notin unread[i]
          /\ max \in [P -> Nat]
          /\ nxt \in [P -> P]
          /\ \A i \in P : (pc[i] = "p6") => nxt[i] # i
          /\ pc \in
              [P -> {"p1", "p2", "p3", "p4", "p5", "p6", "p7", "p8"}]

GG(j, i) == IF j > i THEN num[i] > num[j]
                     ELSE num[i] >= num[j]

After(i, j) ==  /\ num[j] > 0
                /\ \/ pc[i] = "p1"
                   \/ /\ pc[i] = "p2"
                      /\ \/ j \in unread[i]
                         \/ max[i] >= num[j]
                   \/ /\ pc[i] = "p3"
                      /\ max[i] >= num[j]
                   \/ /\ pc[i] \in {"p4", "p5", "p6"}
                      /\ GG(j,i)
                      /\ (pc[i] \in {"p5", "p6"}) => (j \in unread[i])

IInv(i) ==
  /\ (num[i] = 0) <=> (pc[i] \in {"p1", "p2", "p3"})
  /\ flag[i] <=> (pc[i] \in {"p2", "p3", "p4"})
  /\ (pc[i] \in {"p5", "p6"}) =>
        \A j \in (P \ unread[i]) \ {i} : After(j, i)
  /\ /\ (pc[i] = "p6")
     /\ \/ (pc[nxt[i]] = "p2") /\ (i \notin unread[nxt[i]])
        \/ pc[nxt[i]] = "p3"
     => max[nxt[i]] >= num[i]
  /\ (pc[i] \in {"p7", "p8"}) => \A j \in P \ {i} : After(j, i)
Inv == TypeOK /\ \A i \in P : IInv(i)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM GTAxiom  == \A n, m \in Nat : ~ (n > m /\ m > n)
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto dest: nat_less_trans)") }*)

THEOREM GEQAxiom == \A n, m \in Nat : (n = m) \/ n > m \/ m > n
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto elim: nat_less_cases)") }*)

THEOREM GEQTransitive == \A n, m, q \in Nat : n >= m /\ m >= q => n >= q
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto dest: nat_leq_trans)") }*)

THEOREM Transitivity2 == \A n, m, q \in Nat : n > m /\ m >= q => n > q
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto dest: nat_leq_less_trans)") }*)

THEOREM GEQorLT == \A n, m \in Nat : n >= m <=> ~(m > n)
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto simp: nat_not_less[simplified])") }*)

THEOREM NatGEQZero == \A n \in Nat: (n > 0) <=> (n # 0)
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto simp: nat_gt0_not0)") }*)

THEOREM Plus1 == \A n \in Nat: n+1 \in Nat /\ n+1 # 0
  OBVIOUS

THEOREM GGIrreflexive == ASSUME NEW i \in P,
                                NEW j \in P,
                                i # j,
                                num[i] \in Nat,
                                num[j] \in Nat
                         PROVE  ~ (GG(i, j) /\ GG(j, i))
  PROOF OMITTED

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
THEOREM InitImpliesTypeOK == 
  ASSUME Init
  PROVE  TypeOK
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM TypeOKInvariant ==
        ASSUME TypeOK,
               Next
        PROVE  TypeOK'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM InitInv == Init => Inv
PROOF OBVIOUS

=============================================================================
--- tail ---
            /\ UNCHANGED << num, flag, unread, max >>

p6(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p6"
            /\ \/ num[nxt[self]] = 0
               \/ IF self > nxt[self] THEN num[nxt[self]] > num[self]
                                      ELSE num[nxt[self]] >= num[self]
            /\ unread' = [unread EXCEPT ![self] = unread[self] \ {nxt[self]}]
            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p5"]
            /\ UNCHANGED << num, flag, max, nxt >>

p7(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p7"
            /\ TRUE
            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p8"]
            /\ UNCHANGED << num, flag, unread, max, nxt >>

p8(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p8"
            /\ num' = [num EXCEPT ![self] = 0]
            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p1"]
            /\ UNCHANGED << flag, unread, max, nxt >>

p(self) == p1(self) \/ p2(self) \/ p3(self) \/ p4(self) \/ p5(self)
              \/ p6(self) \/ p7(self) \/ p8(self)

Next == (\E self \in P: p(self))
           \/ (* Disjunct to prevent deadlock on termination *)
              ((\A self \in P: pc[self] = "Done") /\ UNCHANGED vars)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars

Termination == <>(\A self \in P: pc[self] = "Done")

\* END TRANSLATION

MutualExclusion == \A i,j \in P : (i # j) => ~ /\ pc[i] = "p7"
                                               /\ pc[j] = "p7"

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
TypeOK == /\ num  \in [P -> Nat]
          /\ flag \in [P -> BOOLEAN]
          /\ unread \in [P -> SUBSET P]
          /\ \A i \in P :
                pc[i] \in {"p2", "p5", "p6"} => i \notin unread[i]
          /\ max \in [P -> Nat]
          /\ nxt \in [P -> P]
          /\ \A i \in P : (pc[i] = "p6") => nxt[i] # i
          /\ pc \in
              [P -> {"p1", "p2", "p3", "p4", "p5", "p6", "p7", "p8"}]

GG(j, i) == IF j > i THEN num[i] > num[j]
                     ELSE num[i] >= num[j]

After(i, j) ==  /\ num[j] > 0
                /\ \/ pc[i] = "p1"
                   \/ /\ pc[i] = "p2"
                      /\ \/ j \in unread[i]
                         \/ max[i] >= num[j]
                   \/ /\ pc[i] = "p3"
                      /\ max[i] >= num[j]
                   \/ /\ pc[i] \in {"p4", "p5", "p6"}
                      /\ GG(j,i)
                      /\ (pc[i] \in {"p5", "p6"}) => (j \in unread[i])

IInv(i) ==
  /\ (num[i] = 0) <=> (pc[i] \in {"p1", "p2", "p3"})
  /\ flag[i] <=> (pc[i] \in {"p2", "p3", "p4"})
  /\ (pc[i] \in {"p5", "p6"}) =>
        \A j \in (P \ unread[i]) \ {i} : After(j, i)
  /\ /\ (pc[i] = "p6")
     /\ \/ (pc[nxt[i]] = "p2") /\ (i \notin unread[nxt[i]])
        \/ pc[nxt[i]] = "p3"
     => max[nxt[i]] >= num[i]
  /\ (pc[i] \in {"p7", "p8"}) => \A j \in P \ {i} : After(j, i)
Inv == TypeOK /\ \A i \in P : IInv(i)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM GTAxiom  == \A n, m \in Nat : ~ (n > m /\ m > n)
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto dest: nat_less_trans)") }*)

THEOREM GEQAxiom == \A n, m \in Nat : (n = m) \/ n > m \/ m > n
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto elim: nat_less_cases)") }*)

THEOREM GEQTransitive == \A n, m, q \in Nat : n >= m /\ m >= q => n >= q
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto dest: nat_leq_trans)") }*)

THEOREM Transitivity2 == \A n, m, q \in Nat : n > m /\ m >= q => n > q
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto dest: nat_leq_less_trans)") }*)

THEOREM GEQorLT == \A n, m \in Nat : n >= m <=> ~(m > n)
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto simp: nat_not_less[simplified])") }*)

THEOREM NatGEQZero == \A n \in Nat: (n > 0) <=> (n # 0)
  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto simp: nat_gt0_not0)") }*)

THEOREM Plus1 == \A n \in Nat: n+1 \in Nat /\ n+1 # 0
  OBVIOUS

THEOREM GGIrreflexive == ASSUME NEW i \in P,
                                NEW j \in P,
                                i # j,
                                num[i] \in Nat,
                                num[j] \in Nat
                         PROVE  ~ (GG(i, j) /\ GG(j, i))
  PROOF OMITTED

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
THEOREM InitImpliesTypeOK == 
  ASSUME Init
  PROVE  TypeOK
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM TypeOKInvariant ==
        ASSUME TypeOK,
               Next
        PROVE  TypeOK'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM InitInv == Init => Inv
PROOF OBVIOUS

=============================================================================

1	------------ MODULE AtomicBakeryWithoutSMT_InitInv ----------------------------
     2	(***************************************************************************)
     3	(* The Atomic Bakery algorithm is a version of the bakery algorithm in     *)
     4	(* which reading and writing of a process's number is an atomic operation. *)
     5	(* The bakery algorithm originally appeared in:                            *)
     6	(*                                                                         *)
     7	(*   Leslie Lamport                                                        *)
     8	(*   A New Solution of Dijkstra's Concurrent Programming Problem           *)
     9	(*   Communications of the ACM 17, 8   (August 1974), 453-455              *)
    10	(*                                                                         *)
    11	(* This PlusCal version of the Atomic Bakery algorithm is one in which     *)
    12	(* variables whose initial values are not used are initialized to          *)
    13	(* arbitrary type-correct values.  It is good for proving correctness but  *)
    14	(* not for model checking, because it produces an unnecessarily large      *)
    15	(* number of reachable states.  If the variables were left uninitialized,  *)
    16	(* the PlusCal translation would initialize them to a particular           *)
    17	(* unspecified value.  This would complicate the proof because it would    *)
    18	(* make the type-correctness invariant more complicated, but it would be   *)
    19	(* efficient to model check.  We could write a version that is both easy   *)
    20	(* to prove and efficient to model check by initializing the variables to  *)
    21	(* particular type-correct values.                                         *)
    22	(*                                                                         *)
    23	(* The proofs in this module were written before TLAPS's SMT backend was   *)
    24	(* implemented, and constitute indeed one of the first proofs ever carried *)
    25	(* out using TLAPS. Much shorter proofs can be obtained using that backend *)
    26	(* -- see module AtomicBakery in the same directory.                      *)
    27	(***************************************************************************)
    28	EXTENDS Naturals, TLAPS
    29	
    30	CONSTANT P
    31	ASSUME PsubsetNat == P \subseteq Nat
    32	
    33	(*********************************************************************
    34	--algorithm AtomicBakery {
    35	variable num = [i \in P |-> 0], flag = [i \in P |-> FALSE];
    36	
    37	process (p \in P)
    38	  variables unread \in SUBSET P, 
    39	            max \in Nat, 
    40	            nxt \in P
    41	{
    42	p1: while (TRUE) {
    43	      unread := P \ {self} ;
    44	      max := 0;
    45	      flag[self] := TRUE;
    46	p2:   while (unread # {}) {
    47	        with (i \in unread) { unread := unread \ {i};
    48	                              if (num[i] > max) { max := num[i]; }
    49	         }
    50	       };
    51	p3:   num[self] := max + 1;
    52	p4:   flag[self] := FALSE;
    53	      unread := P \ {self} ;
    54	p5:   while (unread # {}) {
    55	        with (i \in unread) { nxt := i ; };
    56	        await ~ flag[nxt];
    57	p6:     await \/ num[nxt] = 0
    58	              \/ IF self > nxt THEN num[nxt] > num[self]
    59	                               ELSE num[nxt] \geq num[self];
    60	        unread := unread \ {nxt};
    61	        } ;
    62	p7:   skip ; \* critical section;
    63	p8:  num[self] := 0;
    64	 }}
    65	}
    66	*********************************************************************)
    67	
    68	\* BEGIN TRANSLATION
    69	CONSTANT defaultInitValue
    70	VARIABLES num, flag, pc, unread, max, nxt
    71	
    72	vars == << num, flag, pc, unread, max, nxt >>
    73	
    74	Init == (* Global variables *)
    75	        /\ num = [i \in P |-> 0]
    76	        /\ flag = [i \in P |-> FALSE]
    77	        (* Process p *)
    78	        /\ unread \in [P -> SUBSET P]
    79	        /\ max \in [P -> Nat]
    80	        /\ nxt \in [P -> P]
    81	        /\ pc = [self \in P |-> "p1"]
    82	
    83	p1(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p1"
    84	            /\ unread' = [unread EXCEPT ![self] = P \ {self}]
    85	            /\ max' = [max EXCEPT ![self] = 0]
    86	            /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![self] = TRUE]
    87	            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p2"]
    88	            /\ UNCHANGED << num, nxt >>
    89	
    90	p2(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p2"
    91	            /\ IF unread[self] # {}
    92	                  THEN /\ \E i \in unread[self]:
    93	                            /\ unread' = [unread EXCEPT
    94	                                            ![self] = unread[self] \ {i}]
    95	                            /\ IF num[i] > max[self]
    96	                                  THEN /\ max' = [max EXCEPT
    97	                                                    ![self] = num[i]]
    98	                                  ELSE /\ TRUE
    99	                                       /\ UNCHANGED max
   100	                       /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p2"]
   101	                  ELSE /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p3"]
   102	                       /\ UNCHANGED << unread, max >>
   103	            /\ UNCHANGED << num, flag, nxt >>
   104	
   105	p3(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p3"
   106	            /\ num' = [num EXCEPT ![self] = max[self] + 1]
   107	            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p4"]
   108	            /\ UNCHANGED << flag, unread, max, nxt >>
   109	
   110	p4(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p4"
   111	            /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![self] = FALSE]
   112	            /\ unread' = [unread EXCEPT ![self] = P \ {self}]
   113	            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p5"]
   114	            /\ UNCHANGED << num, max, nxt >>
   115	
   116	p5(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p5"
   117	            /\ IF unread[self] # {}
   118	                  THEN /\ \E i \in unread[self]:
   119	                            nxt' = [nxt EXCEPT
   120	                                      ![self] = i]
   121	                       /\ ~ flag[nxt'[self]]
   122	                       /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p6"]
   123	                  ELSE /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p7"]
   124	                       /\ UNCHANGED nxt

   126	
   127	p6(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p6"
   128	            /\ \/ num[nxt[self]] = 0
   129	               \/ IF self > nxt[self] THEN num[nxt[self]] > num[self]
   130	                                      ELSE num[nxt[self]] >= num[self]
   131	            /\ unread' = [unread EXCEPT ![self] = unread[self] \ {nxt[self]}]
   132	            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p5"]
   133	            /\ UNCHANGED << num, flag, max, nxt >>
   134	
   135	p7(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p7"
   136	            /\ TRUE
   137	            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p8"]
   138	            /\ UNCHANGED << num, flag, unread, max, nxt >>
   139	
   140	p8(self) == /\ pc[self] = "p8"
   141	            /\ num' = [num EXCEPT ![self] = 0]
   142	            /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![self] = "p1"]
   143	            /\ UNCHANGED << flag, unread, max, nxt >>
   144	
   145	p(self) == p1(self) \/ p2(self) \/ p3(self) \/ p4(self) \/ p5(self)
   146	              \/ p6(self) \/ p7(self) \/ p8(self)
   147	
   148	Next == (\E self \in P: p(self))
   149	           \/ (* Disjunct to prevent deadlock on termination *)
   150	              ((\A self \in P: pc[self] = "Done") /\ UNCHANGED vars)
   151	
   152	Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars
   153

   157	
   158	MutualExclusion == \A i,j \in P : (i # j) => ~ /\ pc[i] = "p7"
   159	                                               /\ pc[j] = "p7"

   161	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   162	TypeOK == /\ num  \in [P -> Nat]
   163	          /\ flag \in [P -> BOOLEAN]
   164	          /\ unread \in [P -> SUBSET P]
   165	          /\ \A i \in P :
   166	                pc[i] \in {"p2", "p5", "p6"} => i \notin unread[i]
   167	          /\ max \in [P -> Nat]

   170	          /\ pc \in
   171	              [P -> {"p1", "p2", "p3", "p4", "p5", "p6", "p7", "p8"}]

   174	                     ELSE num[i] >= num[j]
   175	
   176	After(i, j) ==  /\ num[j] > 0
   177	                /\ \/ pc[i] = "p1"
   178	                   \/ /\ pc[i] = "p2"
   179	                      /\ \/ j \in unread[i]
   180	                         \/ max[i] >= num[j]
   181	                   \/ /\ pc[i] = "p3"
   182	                      /\ max[i] >= num[j]
   183	                   \/ /\ pc[i] \in {"p4", "p5", "p6"}
   184	                      /\ GG(j,i)
   185	                      /\ (pc[i] \in {"p5", "p6"}) => (j \in unread[i])
   186	
   187	IInv(i) ==
   188	  /\ (num[i] = 0) <=> (pc[i] \in {"p1", "p2", "p3"})
   189	  /\ flag[i] <=> (pc[i] \in {"p2", "p3", "p4"})
   190	  /\ (pc[i] \in {"p5", "p6"}) =>
   191	        \A j \in (P \ unread[i]) \ {i} : After(j, i)
   192	  /\ /\ (pc[i] = "p6")
   193	     /\ \/ (pc[nxt[i]] = "p2") /\ (i \notin unread[nxt[i]])
   194	        \/ pc[nxt[i]] = "p3"
   195	     => max[nxt[i]] >= num[i]
   196	  /\ (pc[i] \in {"p7", "p8"}) => \A j \in P \ {i} : After(j, i)
   197	Inv == TypeOK /\ \A i \in P : IInv(i)
   198	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   199	
   200	THEOREM GTAxiom  == \A n, m \in Nat : ~ (n > m /\ m > n)
   201	  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto dest: nat_less_trans)") }*)
   202	
   203	THEOREM GEQAxiom == \A n, m \in Nat : (n = m) \/ n > m \/ m > n
   204	  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto elim: nat_less_cases)") }*)
   205	
   206	THEOREM GEQTransitive == \A n, m, q \in Nat : n >= m /\ m >= q => n >= q
   207	  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto dest: nat_leq_trans)") }*)
   208	
   209	THEOREM Transitivity2 == \A n, m, q \in Nat : n > m /\ m >= q => n > q
   210	  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto dest: nat_leq_less_trans)") }*)
   211	
   212	THEOREM GEQorLT == \A n, m \in Nat : n >= m <=> ~(m > n)
   213	  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto simp: nat_not_less[simplified])") }*)
   214	
   215	THEOREM NatGEQZero == \A n \in Nat: (n > 0) <=> (n # 0)
   216	  OBVIOUS (*{ by (isabelle "(auto simp: nat_gt0_not0)") }*)
   217	
   218	THEOREM Plus1 == \A n \in Nat: n+1 \in Nat /\ n+1 # 0
   219	  OBVIOUS
   220	
   221	THEOREM GGIrreflexive == ASSUME NEW i \in P,
   222	                                NEW j \in P,
   223	                                i # j,
   224	                                num[i] \in Nat,
   225	                                num[j] \in Nat
   226	                         PROVE  ~ (GG(i, j) /\ GG(j, i))
   227	  PROOF OMITTED
   228	
   229	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   230	THEOREM InitImpliesTypeOK == 
   231	  ASSUME Init
   232	  PROVE  TypeOK
   233	  PROOF OMITTED
   234	
   235	THEOREM TypeOKInvariant ==
   236	        ASSUME TypeOK,
   237	               Next
   238	        PROVE  TypeOK'
   239	  PROOF OMITTED
   240

item.text: The theorem is just initial-state invariant expansion, so I’ll first try a compact proof using the admitted type lemma.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: AtomicBakeryWithoutSMT_InitInv.tla
@@
 THEOREM InitInv == Init => Inv
-PROOF OBVIOUS
+PROOF
+  BY InitImpliesTypeOK DEF Init, Inv, IInv, After
 
 =============================================================================
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps AtomicBakeryWithoutSMT_InitInv.tla"

item.text:
Implemented and verified the final TLAPS proof.

- Replaced `PROOF OBVIOUS` with a valid proof at `AtomicBakeryWithoutSMT_InitInv.tla:242`.
- Used the admitted lemma `InitImpliesTypeOK` plus definitions `Init`, `Inv`, `IInv`, and `After`.
- Ran `~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps AtomicBakeryWithoutSMT_InitInv.tla`: all obligations proved.
- Ran `python3 check_proof.py AtomicBakeryWithoutSMT_InitInv.tla`: PASS, no cheating detected.
